content.problemStatement:
You are given arrays LLL and RRR, each of size NNN, denoting numbers in base BBB representation.

Count the number of arrays of size NNN such that:


The array denotes a number in base BBB representation;
The number denoted by the array lies between LLL and RRR (both inclusive);
There are exactly KKK distinct elements in the array.
Since the answer may be large, output it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, BBB and KKK.
The second line contains NNN integers, L1,L2,‚Ä¶,LNL_1, L_2, \ldots, L_NL1‚Äč,L2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,LN‚Äč, representing the digits of LLL in base BBB, most significant to least significant.
The third line contains NNN integers, R1,R2,‚Ä¶,RNR_1, R_2, \ldots, R_NR1‚Äč,R2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,RN‚Äč, representing the digits of RRR in base BBB, most significant to least significant.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the count of numbers in base BBB between LLL and RRR with exactly KKK distinct digits.


Since the answer may be large, output it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: The sum of NNN does not exceed 200020002000 over all test cases

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : It can be shown that all arrays [X][X][X] where 234‚Č§X‚Č§1986234 \le X \le 1986234‚Č§X‚Č§1986 satisfy the required conditions, and there are 175317531753 such arrays.
Test Case 2 : It can be proven that no valid array exists
Test Case 3 : There are 243243243 numbers in base-101010 which have exactly 222 distinct digits and lie between 100100100 and 999999999.
